accepted.reason: Matrix transpose loop kernel is a straightforward memory-access optimization target.
Prime-sieve and nth-prime routines provide strong integer-loop and indexing-heavy numeric examples.
Cookie parse/format logic is practical protocol-processing code with robust branching and string handling.
Byte-level serialization primitives are portable low-level routines useful for systems-oriented optimization tasks.
N-body integration loops are classic compute-heavy kernels with clear speedup potential.
Sequence dictionary-building compression logic provides nontrivial string/list state transitions.
In-place insertion sort is a compact baseline algorithmic kernel for correctness/performance tasks.
Chunked multi-file reader control flow demonstrates practical I/O pipeline structure.

skipped.reason: Strongly coupled to NVTX native APIs and domain handles, limiting standalone portability.
Cython compiler/runtime test coverage file rather than a reusable optimization target.
